blocks.body: Questions and Negations helps you build English questions, short answers and negative questions. This section brings together the main patterns with auxiliaries, question types and common answer patterns.

blocks.heading: Question forms
blocks.body:
• Question Forms is the starting page for building English questions.
  • Yes / No Questions and Wh-Questions have their own lessons.
  • Word order in questions is built with auxiliaries: Do you...? Is he...? Can they...?
  • These patterns help you move quickly from a statement to a question.

blocks.heading: Short answers
blocks.body:
• The section includes Answers to Questions for short and full answers.
  • Short answers use the same auxiliary: Yes, I do. / No, they aren't.
  • The page also shows how to answer Wh-questions and negative questions.

blocks.heading: Question tags
blocks.body:
• Question Tags have their own lesson: You like tea, don't you?
  • The focus is on the tag form, polarity and basic intonation.
  • These questions are common for confirmation, checking and friendly conversation.

blocks.heading: Topics in this topic
blocks.body:
TopicLevelDescription
Question FormsA1How to build English questions
Yes / No QuestionsA1General questions with a yes / no answer
Wh-QuestionsA1–A2Questions with who, what, where, when, why, how
Answers to QuestionsA1–A2Short and full answers
Alternative QuestionsA2Choosing between options
Question TagsB1Short tags at the end of a sentence
Negative QuestionsB1Negative questions like Don't you...?

blocks.heading: Negation
blocks.body:
• In this topic, negation appears where you need it for question patterns and short answers.
  • Negative Questions have a separate lesson.
  • Forms like don't / isn't / can't appear throughout the section as part of natural question patterns.

blocks.heading: Learning tips
blocks.body:
🧠

Start with Question Forms, then move to Yes / No Questions and Wh-Questions.

After that, add Answers to Questions, Alternative Questions and Question Tags.

Negative Questions work best after the auxiliary patterns already feel familiar.
